H1项目奖.reason:
Through strong collaboration with key cross-functional (XFN) partners, the team not only delivered a significant GMV breakthrough but also enhanced platform capability and seller confidence in operating on TikTok Shop.
The campaign achieved a remarkable uplift in GMV, marking a clear turning point in monthly business performance across multiple EU markets. More importantly, this growth sustained post-campaign, reflecting improved market fundamentals and increased seller and creator confidence at an early stage of market development.
A major driver of this success was the team's focus on scaling content and supply. In partnership with the Creator Management (CM) and CAI teams, the team launched targeted creator tasks that drove the supply of short videos with sales and significantly increased L2+ creators.
To further optimize the user experience and improve pricing competitiveness, the team worked with Shoptab to refine flash sale product selection and expand strike-through price coverage. This resulted in historically high levels of seller-funded subsidies and improved purchase conversion rates. New features such as the reference price tool and “register on behalf” function were also introduced, simplifying operations and driving better end-to-end conversion.
Additionally, the team collaborated with campaign POCs from POP and FS supply side to strategically boost hero SKUs via Shoptab. These efforts led to record-setting performance for both product with sales during the campaign period. This cross-functional synergy ensured that top-performing products received maximum exposure and delivery reliability in EU5 markets.
Recognizing the need to improve campaign education and seller onboarding, the EU team also co-led the first regional campaign webinar across multiple EU markets in collaboration with local AMs and CMs. The webinar significantly enhanced seller awareness, and product registration coverage for campaign participation rose from 40% to over 56%, laying a strong foundation for future campaign scalability."

These efforts led to record-setting performance for both product with sales during the campaign period. This cross-functional synergy ensured that top-performing products received maximum exposure and delivery reliability in EU5 markets.
Recognizing the need to improve campaign education and seller onboarding, the EU team also co-led the first regional campaign webinar across multiple EU markets in collaboration with local AMs and CMs. The webinar significantly enhanced seller awareness, and product registration coverage for campaign participation rose from 40% to over 56%, laying a strong foundation for future campaign scalability."

Through strong collaboration with key cross-functional (XFN) partners, the team not only delivered a significant GMV breakthrough but also enhanced platform capability and seller confidence in operating on TikTok Shop.
The campaign achieved a remarkable uplift in GMV, marking a clear turning point in monthly business performance across multiple EU markets. More importantly, this growth sustained post-campaign, reflecting improved market fundamentals and increased seller and creator confidence at an early stage of market development.
A major driver of this success was the team's focus on scaling content and supply. In partnership with the Creator Management (CM) and CAI teams, the team launched targeted creator tasks that drove the supply of short videos with sales and significantly increased L2+ creators.
To further optimize the user experience and improve pricing competitiveness, the team worked with Shoptab to refine flash sale product selection and expand strike-through price coverage. This resulted in historically high levels of seller-funded subsidies and improved purchase conversion rates. New features such as the reference price tool and “register on behalf” function were also introduced, simplifying operations and driving better end-to-end conversion.
Additionally, the team collaborated with campaign POCs from POP and FS supply side to strategically boost hero SKUs via Shoptab. These efforts led to record-setting performance for both product with sales during the campaign period. This cross-functional synergy ensured that top-performing products received maximum exposure and delivery reliability in EU5 markets.
Recognizing the need to improve campaign education and seller onboarding, the EU team also co-led the first regional campaign webinar across multiple EU markets in collaboration with local AMs and CMs. The webinar significantly enhanced seller awareness, and product registration coverage for campaign participation rose from 40% to over 56%, laying a strong foundation for future campaign scalability."

From 2024 to 2025, the Germany, France, Italy, and Japan market entry project teams, with resilience and innovation, successfully expanded into the three European countries and Japan, achieving remarkable results in overseas expansion.  
- **Both projects overcame extreme collaboration challenges: **For products alone, they involved over 15 product domains and hundreds of people in cross-continental, cross-time-zone collaboration. Amid multi-team parallel advancement, resources and risks were balanced, and problems were ultimately resolved through efficient collaboration.  
- **Strengths were demonstrated in compliance and market breakthroughs:** In highly regulated Europe, Germany, France, and Italy completed 154 pre-launch compliance decisions and 56 subsequent requirements, achieving localized adaptation of key legislations; Japan addressed the market characteristics of strong offline retail and low consumer acceptance, breaking the deadlock with in-depth localization.  
- **Excellent business results and quality:** The market entry quality of Germany, France, and Italy improved significantly – C-end online issues dropped from 45 in Spain/Ireland (single country) to 27, with no P0 feedback. Germany’s first-month GMV achievement rate reached 570%. The launch of e-commerce models in the Post-MVP phase drove substantial GMV growth, and the mall went fully live 2 weeks ahead of schedule. For Japan, over 12,000 merchants registered in the first month (achievement rate over 300%); first-week GMV exceeded expectations by 10x+, with 135+ features launched. Only 4 P0 issues emerged during UAT, and merchant/influencer onboarding started 2 weeks early. Localization optimizations quickly enhanced user experience.  
Both regions delivered results through targeted localization strategies, setting a benchmark for global market expansion and continuously unlocking business potential.

Amid multi-team parallel advancement, resources and risks were balanced, and problems were ultimately resolved through efficient collaboration.  
- **Strengths were demonstrated in compliance and market breakthroughs:** In highly regulated Europe, Germany, France, and Italy completed 154 pre-launch compliance decisions and 56 subsequent requirements, achieving localized adaptation of key legislations; Japan addressed the market characteristics of strong offline retail and low consumer acceptance, breaking the deadlock with in-depth localization.  
- **Excellent business results and quality:** The market entry quality of Germany, France, and Italy improved significantly – C-end online issues dropped from 45 in Spain/Ireland (single country) to 27, with no P0 feedback. Germany’s first-month GMV achievement rate reached 570%. The launch of e-commerce models in the Post-MVP phase drove substantial GMV growth, and the mall went fully live 2 weeks ahead of schedule. For Japan, over 12,000 merchants registered in the first month (achievement rate over 300%); first-week GMV exceeded expectations by 10x+, with 135+ features launched. Only 4 P0 issues emerged during UAT, and merchant/influencer onboarding started 2 weeks early. Localization optimizations quickly enhanced user experience.  
Both regions delivered results through targeted localization strategies, setting a benchmark for global market expansion and continuously unlocking business potential.

- **Strengths were demonstrated in compliance and market breakthroughs:** In highly regulated Europe, Germany, France, and Italy completed 154 pre-launch compliance decisions and 56 subsequent requirements, achieving localized adaptation of key legislations; Japan addressed the market characteristics of strong offline retail and low consumer acceptance, breaking the deadlock with in-depth localization.  
- **Excellent business results and quality:** The market entry quality of Germany, France, and Italy improved significantly – C-end online issues dropped from 45 in Spain/Ireland (single country) to 27, with no P0 feedback. Germany’s first-month GMV achievement rate reached 570%. The launch of e-commerce models in the Post-MVP phase drove substantial GMV growth, and the mall went fully live 2 weeks ahead of schedule. For Japan, over 12,000 merchants registered in the first month (achievement rate over 300%); first-week GMV exceeded expectations by 10x+, with 135+ features launched. Only 4 P0 issues emerged during UAT, and merchant/influencer onboarding started 2 weeks early. Localization optimizations quickly enhanced user experience.  
Both regions delivered results through targeted localization strategies, setting a benchmark for global market expansion and continuously unlocking business potential.

The Intra-EU Program is a cross-market, cross-functional initiative that transformed TikTok Shop’s operations in Europe from fragmented, country-specific models into an integrated EU5 system. It serves as both a foundational infrastructure upgrade and a repeatable playbook for future expansion into EU20 and other multi-market regions.
1. Strategic Integration & Governance
The project introduced a unified EU-level governance framework, aggregating key seller metrics and applying core strategies (AHR, SPS, Probation) across countries. This shift from country-level to region-level governance enabled platform-wide visibility and scalable seller management.
1. Scalable Product & Experience Design
The team delivered 25+ features in 3 months, spanning 40+ Seller Center pages and 300+ system modules. These reusable components ensured consistent cross-border shopping, promotion, and fulfillment experiences.
By July 5, intra-EU products accounted for 27% of EU5’s L2L GMV, far exceeding expectations. In some smaller markets like Spain, intra-EU GMV reached 44%, enabling fairer exposure for merchants in less mature markets. Creator adoption was equally strong, with 48% of content featuring intra-EU products, and CTR outperforming traditional L2L goods.
1. Infrastructure & Ecosystem Enablement
GNE and CSM teams were instrumental in enabling this growth, through governance logic, EU-wide APIs, ISV onboarding, and key merchant support. These efforts allowed markets to rapidly scale up despite lean resources.
1. Strategic Spillover
The program introduced core concepts—such as “EU as One,” B-side and C-side GMV separation, and “home vs. register country”—which have now been successfully implemented. These innovations will directly support the launch of EU20 and other small-market clusters in the future.
1. Cross-Functional Collaboration
This success would not have been possible without strong partnership across Product, Strategy, GNE, Logistics, Ops, Data, Design, and Research—across both global and regional layers.
Conclusion
This project delivered rapid results and laid the groundwork for long-term EU integration. It exemplifies how clear strategy, deep collaboration, and scalable execution can reshape how TikTok Shop grows across borders.

1. Infrastructure & Ecosystem Enablement
GNE and CSM teams were instrumental in enabling this growth, through governance logic, EU-wide APIs, ISV onboarding, and key merchant support. These efforts allowed markets to rapidly scale up despite lean resources.
1. Strategic Spillover
The program introduced core concepts—such as “EU as One,” B-side and C-side GMV separation, and “home vs. register country”—which have now been successfully implemented. These innovations will directly support the launch of EU20 and other small-market clusters in the future.
1. Cross-Functional Collaboration
This success would not have been possible without strong partnership across Product, Strategy, GNE, Logistics, Ops, Data, Design, and Research—across both global and regional layers.
Conclusion
This project delivered rapid results and laid the groundwork for long-term EU integration. It exemplifies how clear strategy, deep collaboration, and scalable execution can reshape how TikTok Shop grows across borders.

This success would not have been possible without strong partnership across Product, Strategy, GNE, Logistics, Ops, Data, Design, and Research—across both global and regional layers.
Conclusion
This project delivered rapid results and laid the groundwork for long-term EU integration. It exemplifies how clear strategy, deep collaboration, and scalable execution can reshape how TikTok Shop grows across borders.

The Intra-EU Program is a cross-market, cross-functional initiative that transformed TikTok Shop’s operations in Europe from fragmented, country-specific models into an integrated EU5 system. It serves as both a foundational infrastructure upgrade and a repeatable playbook for future expansion into EU20 and other multi-market regions.
1. Strategic Integration & Governance
The project introduced a unified EU-level governance framework, aggregating key seller metrics and applying core strategies (AHR, SPS, Probation) across countries. This shift from country-level to region-level governance enabled platform-wide visibility and scalable seller management.
1. Scalable Product & Experience Design
The team delivered 25+ features in 3 months, spanning 40+ Seller Center pages and 300+ system modules. These reusable components ensured consistent cross-border shopping, promotion, and fulfillment experiences.
By July 5, intra-EU products accounted for 27% of EU5’s L2L GMV, far exceeding expectations. In some smaller markets like Spain, intra-EU GMV reached 44%, enabling fairer exposure for merchants in less mature markets. Creator adoption was equally strong, with 48% of content featuring intra-EU products, and CTR outperforming traditional L2L goods.
1. Infrastructure & Ecosystem Enablement
GNE and CSM teams were instrumental in enabling this growth, through governance logic, EU-wide APIs, ISV onboarding, and key merchant support. These efforts allowed markets to rapidly scale up despite lean resources.
1. Strategic Spillover

H1项目奖.project_name: Project Surge: SMB-managed merchant GMV Max activation
H1项目奖.reason:
Project Background and Goal
* TTS UK was lagging the US in terms of T3 and T2 merchant activation rates on Shop Ads
* In addition, ROI1 Ads were being phased out in favour of ROI 2 [GMV Max], which was launched with a 3-month delay in the UK vs the US
* The GMPT team set an adoption goal for SMB-eligible merchants for the UK to reach parity with the US accounting for the 3-month lag. I.e., for the UK to have the same activation rate at the end of June 2025 as the US had at the end of March.
Up from ~28% end of April.
In addition, there was a secondary goal for T2 merchant activation. 
Action plan: 
* Segmentation of merchant groups by team responsible for the account: TTS Ops; TTS MAI; SMB AM+; SMB HVA
* Set target by segment, unique to the level of interaction with merchants in the specific pool
* Drive completion via segment specific levers:
+ TTS Ops -> Goal per category SME, as well as the TTS Shop Ads team
+ TTS MAI & the SMB growth team -> Missions and touchpoints launched
+ SMB AM+ -> Identified the remaining SMB AM+ merchants yet to activate
+ SMB HVA -> Overlapped with ongoing activation project of T3+
Overall, this was a true cross functional push and effort. Between TTS Ops, as well as SMB & GMPT teams.
Complication: - Initial low uptake after the first month: By end of May, we only moved from ~28% to ~30% activation rate, with very low uplift. Thus, we pivoted and restructure the levers with daily standups, all parties were fully responsible and all hands on deck. E.g., even cold calling and pitching MAI merchants directly.
Result end of June:
* A total of 301 T3+ SMB merchants have been activated in total on GMV Max at the end of June. Bringing us to an activation rate of ~41% (+123 net activations QoQ). A few things to call out: 
* Activated but churned merchants account for another 47 merchants or +6% of additional activations (bringing our total QoQ activations to 170 of 47%). We have kicked off the winback programme with the SMB team to reactivate these merchants in July.
* It worked out that the real US benchmark for SMB managed merchants at the end of March was 35%, which was sizably outperformed.

Thus, we pivoted and restructure the levers with daily standups, all parties were fully responsible and all hands on deck. E.g., even cold calling and pitching MAI merchants directly.
Result end of June:
* A total of 301 T3+ SMB merchants have been activated in total on GMV Max at the end of June. Bringing us to an activation rate of ~41% (+123 net activations QoQ). A few things to call out: 
* Activated but churned merchants account for another 47 merchants or +6% of additional activations (bringing our total QoQ activations to 170 of 47%). We have kicked off the winback programme with the SMB team to reactivate these merchants in July.
* It worked out that the real US benchmark for SMB managed merchants at the end of March was 35%, which was sizably outperformed.

* It worked out that the real US benchmark for SMB managed merchants at the end of March was 35%, which was sizably outperformed.

H1项目奖.project_name: Project Surge: SMB-managed merchant GMV Max activation
H1项目奖.reason:
Project Background and Goal
* TTS UK was lagging the US in terms of T3 and T2 merchant activation rates on Shop Ads
* In addition, ROI1 Ads were being phased out in favour of ROI 2 [GMV Max], which was launched with a 3-month delay in the UK vs the US
* The GMPT team set an adoption goal for SMB-eligible merchants for the UK to reach parity with the US accounting for the 3-month lag. I.e., for the UK to have the same activation rate at the end of June 2025 as the US had at the end of March.
Up from ~28% end of April.
In addition, there was a secondary goal for T2 merchant activation. 
Action plan: 
* Segmentation of merchant groups by team responsible for the account: TTS Ops; TTS MAI; SMB AM+; SMB HVA
* Set target by segment, unique to the level of interaction with merchants in the specific pool
* Drive completion via segment specific levers:
+ TTS Ops -> Goal per category SME, as well as the TTS Shop Ads team
+ TTS MAI & the SMB growth team -> Missions and touchpoints launched
+ SMB AM+ -> Identified the remaining SMB AM+ merchants yet to activate
+ SMB HVA -> Overlapped with ongoing activation project of T3+
Overall, this was a true cross functional push and effort. Between TTS Ops, as well as SMB & GMPT teams.
Complication: - Initial low uptake after the first month: By end of May, we only moved from ~28% to ~30% activation rate, with very low uplift. Thus, we pivoted and restructure the levers with daily standups, all parties were fully responsible and all hands on deck. E.g., even cold calling and pitching MAI merchants directly.
Result end of June:
* A total of 301 T3+ SMB merchants have been activated in total on GMV Max at the end of June. Bringing us to an activation rate of ~41% (+123 net activations QoQ). A few things to call out: 
* Activated but churned merchants account for another 47 merchants or +6% of additional activations (bringing our total QoQ activations to 170 of 47%). We have kicked off the winback programme with the SMB team to reactivate these merchants in July.
* It worked out that the real US benchmark for SMB managed merchants at the end of March was 35%, which was sizably outperformed.

H1项目奖.project_name: Expedited Launch of E-com Recommendation Systems in Newly Launched European Markets
H1项目奖.reason:
In H1, during the EU new market expansion, a cross-functional team from CAI, Live Ops, Recommendation Product, Rec Algorithm, and DS faced intense challenges under tight timelines. Their delivery speed outpaced that of the US market, laying strong technical foundations for future launches in Mexico, Brazil, and more. During this time, the team also completed the migration of NO data center infrastructure, overcoming complex tech and resource hurdles.
CAI and Live Ops teammates worked closely with frontline teams to quickly identify key content distribution issues—such as cold start problems and lower traffic in livestreams compared to main platform videos. At launch, the team already implemented several critical improvements still in progress elsewhere, like separate main queue + cold start queue for G1, and adaptations for mixed content feeds.
Recommendation Product and Algorithm teams deeply analyzed root causes and tested simulation models to validate hypotheses. Multiple strategy iterations successfully improved both rec accuracy and system efficiency. DS provided data support for diagnosis and strategy optimization.
Results included:
  ES/IE all GMV AB +31%, rec-video GMV AB +112%, VV +101%
  DE/FR/IT all GMV AB +64%, rec-video VV +367.61%, rec-video GMV AB +292.57%
  rec-live VV +97.26%, rec-live GMV AB +27.31%
  Cold-start video issues fixed: % of videos with <150 VV in 24h dropped from 50%+ to 30%+
  G1 queue strategy reintroduced to address livestream traffic drop post-pinning
These improvements showed the team's strength in solving complex problems and driving innovation under pressure.

Strong business results:
Drove +88% C-end GMV uplift with 117% target attainment across EU5 BFCM, while delivering a record-breaking €14.8M single-day GMV peak that remains unbeaten to date.
Develop repeatable mechanisms:
Built and validated scalable, repeatable growth engines—anchored on creator-led short videos, campaign core product pool, reinforced by high-impact conversion levers (free shipping + price competitiveness), and powered by data-driven operations—unlocking step-change improvements in both efficiency and scale.
Building up product & capability:
Scaled the “EU as One” model to ensure consistent, high-quality cross-market supply, while continuously evolving C-end product capabilities—establishing a structurally stronger foundation for conversion and long-term growth.

Scaled the “EU as One” model to ensure consistent, high-quality cross-market supply, while continuously evolving C-end product capabilities—establishing a structurally stronger foundation for conversion and long-term growth.

In the early stage of the Japan market launch, NPS feedback and operational monitoring identified a high proportion of non-localized content. A dedicated country-level initiative was established, led by GNE and BizOps, in collaboration with DS, Product, Algo, Legal, PR, GR, Advertising, and other cross-functional teams to address the issue systematically.
After more than one quarter of focused governance, the share of non-localized content in total VV decreased from 27% to below 3% and remained stable; the deboost rate of newly submitted content dropped from 57% to 20%. The team categorized the issue into three key areas—non-Japanese language, foreign-context content, and “plastic Japanese”—and developed corresponding models for governance. Through continuous optimization, recall of the most complex “plastic Japanese” model improved by 21pp (67%→81.09%), while coordinated manual evaluation and algorithm refinement controlled the false positive rate at around 2%. As governance stabilized, the strategy evolved from content-level controls to more precise account-level governance, achieving over 95% accuracy. A whitelist process was established to proactively protect more than 2,500 creators. In parallel, the team partnered with PR, GR, and Legal to strengthen creator education and reduce repeated violations.
Within H2, the project significantly improved content quality and user experience, while establishing a scalable governance framework that balances localization, trust, and growth, providing a replicable model for other high-sensitivity markets.

H2项目奖.project_name: E-commerce Short Video Growth Based on Effective Supply
H2项目奖.reason:
Since H2 2025, short-video viewership in EU4 increased while GMV growth lagged. Business and Recommendation teams identified two issues: growth was driven by low-quality or abandoned content that failed to generate “effective supply,” and traffic allocation lacked supply elasticity. The teams redefined “effective supply” as the supply-side North Star, focusing cross-functional efforts on expanding it and improving its GMV leverage.
Daily effective submissions increased from 18,400 in July to 55,400 in December (3x growth). Governance and Recommendation teams reduced exposure to low-quality content, lowering EU5 low-quality share by 4.85pt to 8.54%. With the groundwork of content quality initiatives, In Q4, recommendation team prioritized high GPM and high-engagement, high-transaction content. Our reversal tests confirmed a +32% surge in EU4 video GMV, contributing a massive +15.6% incremental GMV to EU TTS.
The project delivered measurable H2 growth and established a scalable mechanism aligning supply governance and traffic allocation. Teams shared ownership and collaborated efficiently to amplify impact.

The teams redefined “effective supply” as the supply-side North Star, focusing cross-functional efforts on expanding it and improving its GMV leverage.
Daily effective submissions increased from 18,400 in July to 55,400 in December (3x growth). Governance and Recommendation teams reduced exposure to low-quality content, lowering EU5 low-quality share by 4.85pt to 8.54%. With the groundwork of content quality initiatives, In Q4, recommendation team prioritized high GPM and high-engagement, high-transaction content. Our reversal tests confirmed a +32% surge in EU4 video GMV, contributing a massive +15.6% incremental GMV to EU TTS.
The project delivered measurable H2 growth and established a scalable mechanism aligning supply governance and traffic allocation. Teams shared ownership and collaborated efficiently to amplify impact.

H2个人奖.reason: 年末大型キャンペーンにおいて、TSP と密に連携し、年末のユーザー購買トレンドを踏まえた季節商品を多数企画・提案。季節商品において最も多くの採択実績を持つ AM として、最終的に GMV を +400％ 伸長させ、商品 GMV の大幅な成長に大きく貢献しました。 During the year-end promotion period, he closely collaborated with TSP. Based on the shopping trends of the end-of-year users, he submitted seasonal products and successfully had the largest number of them selected as AMs. The final GMV of seasonal products achieved a explosive growth of +400%, making an important contribution to the significant increase in the overall GMV of products.

年末大型キャンペーン期間中に 2.3M GMV を創出し、まさに GMV Driver と言える成果を達成。担当セラーのライブ配信エントリー率が高く、対応スピードも非常に迅速。キャンペーン終盤でのアグレシッブな目標達成に向け、追加の GMV 成長に大きく貢献しました。  During the year-end promotion period, he contributed a total of 2.3M GMV, earning the title of the GMV driver. The live streaming rooms it was responsible for had a high registration rate and responded promptly. In the final stage of the event, when the aggressive goals were being pushed forward, he continuously drove and contributed additional GMV growth.

Lexa spearheaded the highest registration and sample approval rates to date, while proactively creating enablement materials (including a dedicated HNE registration/FAQ one-pager) to drive awareness and adoption. Her collaborative, forward-thinking approach ensured smooth execution and was key to delivering a successful and seamless Black Friday for HNE.

Helped Beauty hit +173% uplift and 110% completion on Day 1 through strong pre-campaign stock, content, and registration support. Hazel was the BFCM SME and quarterbacked the entire campaign effort which culminated in beauty exceeding stretch BFCM goals. She planned for the Nov event from August onwards, articulating assortment bets sellers should make and running efficient daily check ins to support execution

quarterbacked the fashion BFCM effort, breaking down campaign targets at seller level and helped achieve +107% target completion. Yancey ran very efficient check ins, highlighting seller performance at a daily level and alerting AMs to take immediate remedial actions, In addition, she also drove 5 T5s achieving +60% growth across these T5s.

Joanna has been leading the FMCG Black Friday affiliate initiatives to drive samples, content supply & affiliate GMV growth - Joanna has truly demonstrated always day 1 through testing new initiatives. Automating outstanding L1.5+ creator sample requests daily for BDs and AMs to drive up sample approval rate; as well as leveraging new TAP campaigns, leaderboards, tasks and affiliate tools to maximise content output. Joanna has also leveraged some of the high impacting initiatives used for FMCG industry merchants and collaborated with MAI team to share tactics to widen the impact.

Freya led end-to-end content strategy, actively monitoring in-campaign supply and risk while continuously partnering with Creator teams to develop and scale new tasks - not only during the 11-day Black Friday period, but also across subsequent campaigns in December,setting method with DS to track daily video and new video volume, and achieve a huge growth of +27%, significantly outperforming Summer Sale (+4%) and BFCM24 trend and boost BFCM sales. In collaboration with Seth, she delivered a record-breaking number of Mini-KLS sessions with $8M committed in GMV, significantly contributing to live performance and overall campaign success.

As the PM for Black Friday Campaign, has been in charge of the overall campaign preparation since July, covering daily monitoring and coordination until the final launch. She developed phased goals and priorities, including target budget, assortment strategy, scene strategy, and collaboration with industry teams & cross-functional XFN partners. Throughout the campaign, she devoted extensive efforts to intensive communication and proactive coordination to ensure overall progress. Thanks to her dedication, the campaign achieved record-breaking results on Black Friday with both overall platform and L2L hitting all-time highs, achieving a 109% overall completion rate and +74% uplift.

Yajie ranked highest in GMV during BFCM: her sellers contributed USD 1,466K to BFCM GMV, 3x the average GMV/AM in DE. She not only outperformed during BFCM but also finished November full month successfully at 170% scorecard achievement

No.1 in EU 5, in terms of Ads revenue, totaling $406,036, achieving 152% PoP growth with 18.8% of TR , effectively supporting GMV uplift and campaign efficiency. No.2 in EU5,  in terms of total GMV of $2,165,590, representing a 102.6% increase vs. pre-Black Friday period (Nov 10–20).  Core merchants increased from 20 T3 in October to 23 T3 in November and merchant up level: 2 new T5 and 5 new T4 merchants. Worked closely with Campaign, Shoptab, Lives, CM teams, GNE, and SX teams to align resources, streamline processes, and ensure smooth execution of multi-team plans.

During BFCM, Gerard delivered outstanding business impact by contributing $888K GMV, accounting for 28% of total FMCG industry GMV. He proactively guided sellers to launch high-performing FMCG-exclusive F&B bundles (e.g., lote amigos and lote Black Friday), which gained strong traction among customers and creators.  As the FMCG Live POC, he organized and supported 5+ top live sessions with creators and sellers in close collaboration with the central live team, driving an 800% increase in total team live GMV. Multiple sellers, including V-Gummies and La Despensa del Casar, generated $10K+ self-live GMV during BFCM. Gerard demonstrated strong ownership and collaboration by working cross-functionally with creator teams and CM to deliver 3+ top creator live sessions.  He operated with a hands-on, results-driven mindset, communicated clearly across stakeholders, and consistently enabled sellers and creators to execute effectively in a fast-paced peak period, contributing to both short-term performance and long-term capability building.

Chiara demonstrated an individual contribution during BFCM, acting as a IT Campaign POC between the Campaign team and the Italy AM organization. She effectively bridged the two teams by designing, scaling, and standardizing key operating mechanisms that directly supported campaign execution and risk mitigation. Chiara created and owned a weekly-updated campaign tracking doc and led recurring syncs with AMs to share progress, surface constraints, and drive timely actions . She proactively set up category-level working groups between AM and Campaign teams to overachieve critical backstops in product registration (e.g., GNE eligibility, Free Shipping eligibility). She also delivered BFCM campaign webinars to more than 200 sellers across AM-managed and MAI, increasing seller readiness and execution quality. One week before BFCM and during the campaign, she shared daily performance vs. targets, enabling AMs to course-correct in real time and stay focused on priority goals. Her user-focused and execution-driven approach delivered strong results: GMV target rate +116%, 61% Product Registration GMV (with 42% L2L) (+1bps vs target), Live GMV +99% uplift, and 73% Live GMV coverage (+13bps vs target)

During the Black Friday period, Miya was primarily responsible for the overall creators task and outreach system, as well as the execution of the new Prize Sharing project. Throughout the campaign, she demonstrated robust project management expertise by optimizing budget allocations daily based on real-time task data. Her efforts ultimately led to a significant breakthrough (leap) in performance and L1.5+ uplevels

Jin step out as a strategy and analytics role for the 2025 Black Friday campaign, built timely tracking and seller tier-up plans from the ground up. Key contributions included: - Data Infrastructure & Performance Tracking: Built all-in-one datasets and dashboards to enable daily and hourly OKR tracking, support timely optimization of subsidy intensity and target cohorts while maintaining budget control.  - Merchant Segmentation Strategy: Developed a segmentation framework based on merchant behavior and GMV performance, enabling differentiated subsidy strategies / content strategy to maximize efficiency with limited resources.

Nora is the Affiliate POC in MAI, where during BFCM she demonstrated strong ownership and cross-functional execution. Result in $9.46M BFCM Affiliate GMV (+102%), with sample GMV +98% contributing 37% of MAI Affiliate GMV. - All channel promotion on sample shipping: She designed BFCM seller incentives to accelerate MAI free sample supply, leveraged all incubation channels (Outreach, Missions, Mass Account Managers), and iterated outreach based on seller feedback, resulting in a +13% MoM uplift in MAI samples shipped. - Creative personalized emails with AI tools: She collaborated with DS to pilot an AI-powered personalized matchmaking email covering 400+ EU sellers, delivering positive A/B GMV uplift and creating a scalable innovation path. She also partnered with Mass Account Managers to launch the BFCM Affiliate Webinar, achieving 471 seller attendees, 27-min avg. watch time, and NPS >75.

As POC of UK Shoptab’s Black Friday campaign, Kailin achieved outstanding results in this Black Friday promotion: the campaign mechanics and strategies drove a total of 22% of Shoptab incremental GMV. BXGY, the key campaign mechanic, went through multiple iterations ahead of Black Friday, which solidified a mechanism to maximize GMV and monetary efficiency. Within just 3 days of its launch, it contributed 14% of the incremental GMV while maintaining a highly competitive ROI.  From Culture & Ways of Working perspective: , with the strengths of agile responsiveness, meticulous attention to details and efficient collaboration, she tackled the challenge of the tight launch timeline for the new campaign mechanic BMRM. By analyzing and breaking down full-funnel metrics and conducting a horizontal benchmarking against BXGY, she completed three rapid business iterations and finalised a tailored solution for Black Friday. This enabled the quick optimization and improvement of the mechanic’s performance, delivering strong support for the Black Friday campaign.

Ensure BFCM content and creator protection through multiple XFN teams, including TnS/BRIC/TRECO/BI. Provided a smooth BFCM livestream week.   Quickly handled issues raised by the biz ops team, identified enforcement sources, and provided timely feedback on correct penalties. Handled and resolved 40+ escalation cases, initiated 10+ escalations to XFN, with no livestream disruption.

To ensure that all major promotions can succeed in terms of both experience and business results by the end of the year, Xudong collaborated deeply with Ops and implemented several key measures: 1) Continuously optimize the fulfillment experience of promotions, while taking into account the feelings of both merchants and buyers; 2) Design and implement the core AHR governance project, and manage the impact of promotions through phased control; 3) Promote the joint construction of GNE × logistics SOP to efficiently handle abnormal orders during promotions. Ultimately, despite a significant increase in order volume, the fulfillment experience remained stable (with LDR < 2%, and the after-sales indicators being basically the same as those during non-promotion periods).  In addition to handling his own policy-related work, Xudong also demonstrated a strong sense of responsibility and a high level of dedication to "taking one more step". When the colleague in charge of the content scenario was temporarily absent, even though his own workload was already extremely heavy, he still voluntarily took over the content domain and continuously resolved the issues of traffic and content shortages throughout the entire promotion period, ensuring the smooth progress of the promotion.

整个黑五期间pop泡比上升明显，包裹装大箱后重量由20kg/箱降低至14kg/箱左右，泡比升高30%；结合前端销售超预测，各国货量达成：DE 143%；FR 150%；IT 244%；ES 200%，成本及时效遇到双重压力： 广明通过对服务商预报并锁定旺季pop各国固定货量，提前与大b段服务商锁定旺季整月价卡，旺季期间价卡对比淡季成本仅上涨5%； 25年黑五期间欧洲空运市场超预期涨价，涨价幅度8～15rmb/kg，且pop各国单量均超过预测，泡比上升导致服务商干线成本升高约20%；大b段服务商只愿意承接报价时承诺的货量，超过提前预报的货量需要跟随干线市场运价涨价8～15rmb/kg。广明积极和大b段服务商协商，首先要求保证提货时效，避免仓库生产后积压货物；再次了解到大b段服务商的痛点问题主要是泡比超过日常水位，以及货量爆发超过预期；广明针对问题提出以下解决方案： 1.设定合理泡比系数，超过合理泡比系数的货物允许大b段服务商申请泡重计费，但是实施周期是旺季结束后，以保证旺季成本不再额外上涨； 2.针对超预期爆发的货量，平台by天预报未来三天单量/货量及实际生产分单的单量，配合服务商尽可能根据准确的货量预报来采购干线舱位，以保证成本和时效达标； 最终保证整个黑五期间出货顺畅，成本对比淡季成本仅上涨5%； 同时daily跟进大b段空运和异常情况：黑五期间突发欧洲机场口岸&尾程派送罢工等情况，结合pop各国单量爆发超过预期水位，服务商履约压力非常大，广明根据货量爆发及欧洲各国市场情况实施以下动作： EU干线市场在旺季期间遇到两次影响较大的罢工：分别是LGG口岸罢工及意大利全国罢工，罢工信息确认后，首先广明与大b段服务商灵活协调注入口岸及干线航班，避开LGG口岸罢工期间注入，以保障干线时效，其次对意大利尾程服务商施加压力争取罢工后的周末加班处理积压货物，以保证尾程时效未超标；最终在货量超预测，叠加突发本地罢工及罢工后货站拥堵等情况下全链路时效达成； FS 西班牙负责人：黑五期间成本达成，时效体验均达成，并在单量持续超预测第一时间调整卡转整车DAILY转运，提升时效27小时以上； 同时作为EU8新开国PM完成了整体准备工作，目前顺利进入开发阶段；

Yang Xudong joined the company in August and actively studied and mastered the business content. As a newcomer, he successfully handled multiple challenges in various regions during the Black Friday promotion: The quantity of UK POP exceeded expectations and the bubble ratio deteriorated severely, resulting in suppliers requesting limited orders and facing the risk of insufficient transportation capacity; The overall market freight rates in the EU direction skyrocketed (more than 27% above the budget price), making it difficult to achieve the budget; In the JP direction, the last-mile service provider Sora was affected by the price increase of Black Cat and 100% bubble recording, with a large number of packages flooding in, causing severe congestion, and major B suppliers requested limited orders or price increases to change the last-mile plan. Facing various unexpected problems, Xudong responded quickly, communicating closely with suppliers in each region every day. Under the premise of maintaining transportation capacity, he negotiated and settled the quotations to stabilize costs and achieve the target of cost achievement. 1. Assist UK and EU suppliers in exploring low-cost trunk transportation capacity in the market to solve the problem of suppliers difficulty in accepting orders and lack of transportation capacity guarantee, stabilize their loss expectations, and ensure that the limited order range does not affect our daily performance; 2. In response to the congestion of the JP last-mile service, respond quickly, prepare different last-mile scenarios for procurement and promote negotiation and pricing in advance, stabilize the suppliers confidence, and also secure more reasonable freight rates and the cooperation of the suppliers. 3. For the situation where the bubble ratio in the UK, JP, and EU directions deteriorated by nearly 40%, collaborate with operations and suppliers to continuously communicate, monitor the bubble ratio daily, update and adjust the billing logic in a timely manner, ultimately maintaining the cost of EU major B suppliers unchanged, and slightly increasing the costs of UK and JP. At the same time, visit several core suppliers for cooperation expectation management, encouraging the suppliers to actively support the priority shipment of Tiktok goods during the peak season. After the promotion ended, due to the severe decline in cargo volume, Xudong collaborated with operations and UK suppliers to promote special negotiations for BSA loss compensation fees, achieving a reduction of nearly 5 million in default costs, making a positive contribution to the successful conclusion of this peak season promotion business.

As a product-side POC for the year-end promotion event in JP, under the circumstances of extremely limited resources and the lack of experience in SS promotion in Japan, we successfully coordinated all parties, clarified the activity strategy, and led the completion of Japans first SS-level event - the year-end promotion. The GMV soared by 89% and the completion rate reached 124%. The main contributions include: - Strategy and demand implementation: Despite the mismatch in time schedules, we proactively guided the operations to complete the strategy and product selection, focusing on "driving GMV growth through content and expanding conversion efficiency", using live streaming as the core driving force, and coordinating the multi-product domains of recommendation, search, and transactions to handle it. We collaborated with the middle platform and regional products to complete the requirement analysis for the year-end promotion in Japan, and implemented local activities such as rankings, gift bags, and pre-sale coupons, as well as 4 main resource positions and 33 differentiated and 89 global requirements for local deployment. - Activity support and education: Overall, through "preparation in advance + on-site值守 + comprehensive review"， we ensured stability and risk controllability during the rapid growth of the business. We completed 4 full-process product guarantees， demonstrating strong execution and resilience. A total of 85 issues were identified in advance， and we ultimately achieved zero online incidents during the event period， and promoted the consolidation of marketing information and the optimization of key pain points in the products.

Neva was responsible for marketing the C product during this Black Friday event. They continuously explored various aspects such as marketing strategies, expression methods, and overall atmosphere creation, fully leveraging the promotional leverage and significantly boosting the growth of core indicators of the overall market. By using post-coupon prices and the BMRM store gameplay as the core product capabilities, combined with the all-channel year-round low-price tags and the enhanced version of the big promotion waistband, through a strong visual atmosphere, the price advantage of the products was prominently highlighted, successfully driving a 4.15% increase in UK GMV, 1.29% increase in EU GMV, 2.59% increase in AOV, and making a substantial contribution to the overall market growth. They actively innovated based on the local business strategy and achieved clear benefits. They were the first to explore and implement the "post-coupon price" capability in the EU & UK regions, converting the originally scattered "coupon + product promotion" combination into a clear "in-hand low price" mental perception for users, significantly improving the conversion efficiency and promoting a 1.29% increase in overall market GMV/u, and a 1.43% increase in store GMV/u. At the same time, this exploration accumulated reusable pre-requisite experience for the subsequent launch of the post-coupon price in US and other markets, effectively enhancing the regional product influence and methodological accumulation. Under high-pressure conditions, they demonstrated strong collaboration and execution capabilities, efficiently implementing multiple urgent plans during the Black Friday period, effectively addressing the urgent needs of the business. In response to sudden issues such as banner failure in the FS promotion event, they promptly coordinated with the R&D / promotion XFN teams to respond quickly, completing the design and launch of temporary solutions within a very short time, effectively ensuring the smooth progress of the promotion event and providing key support to the FS team.

As the engineering owner for the European sales campaign, xiaoyuan song organized and drove the successful delivery of the project, demonstrating courage in pursuing excellence and strong execution. In the early phase of the campaign, xiaoyuan proactively worked with the PM team to sort out region-specific requirements, coordinated US/regional colleague to support the project, set up bi-weekly meetings to align on progress, and surfaced risks in a timely manner.For this year’s Black Friday campaign in Europe, the core activities differed from those in the US. xiaoyuan quickly identified the differences, aligned with the product team, and jointly pushed for bold optimization experiments tailored to the new European activities. During the campaign, xiaoyuan provided timely technical assessments for issues discovered in reviews and iterated and launched fixes quickly, which, according to A/B test data, brought incremental GMV gains.In addition, the lessons learned from the Black Friday campaign were applied to the Japan Thanks Sale Stellar campaign. As this was the first time Europe led a sales Campaign, these experiences also laid a solid foundation for the smooth execution of future campaigns.

Hearsh is the key contributor for Project Ignite during BCFM.  Policy doc: [GEC-RM-UK] Project Ignite - Q425 Top Seller Growth Acceleration Policy  Results review: [GEC-RM-UK] Project Ignite Review   Project Ignite is a new JBP like program during BCFM (similar to POPs 亿元俱乐部), where we signed contract with top merchants and reward them with 1-2% of GMV incentives based on their incremental GMV growth vs the base line.   The project had great performance during Q4 - it drove 34 merchants with a total incremental GMV of ~$15.7M during BFCM period, with only 423K spending, achieving an estimated AA ROAS of ~37, and ST AA ROI of 2.14  Based on the success of this first trial, we will expand this program in 2026 and cover more merchants / increase the scale of the program, we will also develop AB test for more accurate ROI estimation.

Dan, as 25 BFCM project DS poc, has supported EMEA BFCM for two years, coordinating 40+ DS & DE across strategy and data infrastructure. Deeply supported EU’s first BFCM across 5 countries and 4 time zones (117% target, +88% uplift), UK target achieved 109% (+74% uplift). All markets reached record Peak Day GMV. End-to-End Execution & Enablement: ① Pre-BFCM, built real-time and offline dashboards, integrated multi-domain data into DS DataHub, ensured data timeliness, and trained 50+ ops users. ② During-BFCM, Coordinated on-site DS support enabling fast decision-making, and standardized daily monitoring assets reused in JP mega campaigns. ③ Post-BFCM, delivered clear recommendations on strategies to scale, iterate, or sunset Strategy Impact & Reusability: ① Supported multiple optimization of subsidy strategy with validated AB gains in the UK Campaign, and high subsidy-elasticity product strategies were applied in BFCM; ② Identified opportunity categories and core products based on- and off-platform data to support UK BFCM merchant registration and inventory preparation; ③ Delivered EU5 registration policy and subsidy strategy analysis, registration GMV coverage 60%+ and enabled country-level subsidy differentiation during BFCM.

Supporting the full-chain deep review of the Tokutoku Thanks Sale, a year-end promotion of JP, the system comprehensively analyzed the GMV uplift of various strategy experiments across all dimensions, and accurately identified the product shelves and live events that were significantly driven by subsidies, helping to solidify the methodological foundation for the localization of large-scale promotions in the Japanese market. In the product shelf diagnosis, through quantitative research on the stratification of products, it was confirmed that the registered products for the event were the core drivers of growth (contributing 67% of the incremental GMV, with an uplift of 151%, approximately three times that of non-registered products). Among them, the core product shelves (best-selling products + seasonal products) achieved a 227% uplift, becoming the key driver of growth. In the live streaming analysis, the driving value of KLS and Live Challenge for different梯队 live streaming rooms was clarified， and the strategic positioning of the brand live streaming room "high exposure， low conversion， and high trust" was defined based on data. In the mall analysis， a comprehensive diagnosis was conducted from dimensions such as overall performance， conversion links， core product shelves， marketing atmosphere， and promotion methods， and marketing strategy suggestions were extracted to support the scientific allocation of subsequent promotion resources.

Yu Kangfei, as a designer specializing in large-scale promotion marketing, is capable of quickly grasping the business requirements of large-scale promotions and coordinating cross-team collaboration. He independently takes charge of various levels and types of large-scale promotion activities, fully demonstrating his professional visual skills and communication abilities. He has made outstanding contributions in large-scale promotion marketing design projects. He deeply participated in the preliminary exploration and outcome production of the Black Friday promotion skin scheme, and followed up throughout the implementation of the scheme to ensure a strong atmosphere perception throughout the promotion chain and a high-quality user experience. Through immersive atmosphere creation, he improved the conversion efficiency of various product categories. Subsequently, he followed up on the core data performance of Black Friday and extracted key experiences to form a special analysis summary. He led the exploration of the early strategies for the 12th large-scale promotion and the planning, design, and output of the C-end scheme, actively coordinating with various business domains to promote the configuration and development connection of the scheme, and continuously following up on the entire project process to ensure the quality of implementation. He focused on creating a strong atmosphere perception throughout the promotion chain and ensuring a high-quality user experience, effectively boosting the conversion efficiency of various product categories. Subsequently, he will systematically follow up on the core data performance of the promotion, extract key experiences and optimization directions, and form a structured special analysis summary to provide reusable practical support for the iterative optimization of subsequent promotion activities. He deeply supported the B-level large-scale promotion activities of L2L industry brands, responsible for the output of 13 promotion design schemes - from requirement decomposition, visual strategy formulation to implementation. He fully controlled the design direction and quality standards throughout the process. At the same time, he coordinated with the BPO team to clarify the design execution requirements and followed up on the progress, solved the implementation problems, and ultimately ensured that all promotion design tasks were delivered on schedule according to the nodes, effectively supporting the marketing rhythm of the event. Mid-year promotion, Black Friday, and December promotion: He was responsible for the visual design and creative design schemes for the dynamic point-of-view animations during the promotion period, ensuring the display effect of key design points. Through the design of materials, he improved business conversion. He actively communicated with the main platform to promote the implementation of the design scheme and followed up on the data to provide data support for the subsequent design direction. The experimental conclusion of the main platform scenario - Tab icon alienation during the promotion period, emphasizing the icon expression, was consistent with the conclusion of the mid-year promotion experiment. The version with pure text alienation and text + graphics achieved the best effect. It has been agreed to push the full version v2, and the alienation will be implemented throughout the Black Friday climax period. The strong reach scene on the homepage: The experiment of the coupon pop-up animation adopted two schemes - the pre-box animation and the rising animation of the coupon number. To avoid the impact of performance differences on the experiment, this animation was implemented using front-end code. The experimental conclusion is consistent with that of the mid-year promotion. The rising animation of the coupon number is the best, and the full CRM dynamic construction has been pushed. The conversion efficiency of dynamic CRM is significantly higher than that of static, with a GPM of $17.1 VS $13.93, compared to the past four weeks, the CTR was 9.53% (+42%), during the Black Friday period, the average daily GMV of the CRM channel was 370,000, compared to the baseline period, it increased by 155%, the GMV of the overall market accounted for 4.31% (peak period 5.12%), and the incremental GMV of AB was +2.93%. In collaboration with the main platform, an e-commerce split project was launched, and after the promotion on Black Friday, the daily contribution of e-commerce GMV reached 19,500. Compared to the old split method, the conversion scale increased by 28 times. After the Black Friday, the scale was expanded to bring continuous benefits. The selection of products, numerical strategies, and traffic entry were continuously optimized. After the Black Friday, the average daily GMV has increased to 42,600, and the split method has been fully rolled out (the old method was 0.8, not meeting the standard). The user experience was smooth, with no obvious customer complaint feedback. C-end product design upgrade: With the core goal of "establishing a highly recognizable brand communication and creating a sensory difference from competitors", we explore the upgrade of design language, participate in brainstorming meetings for design language exploration, combine brand tone and product characteristics, and collaborate with the team to clearly define the core design direction. Based on the discussion results, produce the "Infinite" visual language solution, and lead the completion of the full presentation of the solution within a short period of time (including core visual symbols, application norms, scene extensions, etc.). Promotion competition research: In-depth research on core competitors and output strategy suggestions: Lead the analysis of competing products for domestic mid-year promotions, Black Friday, and December promotions, focusing on domestic platforms (Pinduoduo, JD) and cross-border platforms (Temu, SHEIN). From multiple dimensions such as promotion strategies (such as discount mechanisms, coupon gameplay), user interaction (such as coupon collection paths, activity entry redirection), and visual expression (such as core information presentation, atmosphere creation), break down and extract 9 major field differences, providing actionable direction suggestions for business strategies. Standardized presentation of research results: Design research table framework, optimize dimension classification and conclusion presentation logic, improve document reading efficiency through visual layout, and shorten the time to obtain core conclusions. Team collaboration alignment: Organize a competition research results alignment meeting, synchronize research conclusions with business insights, promote the design and product teams to reach consensus on the core direction, and reduce cognitive biases in subsequent collaboration.
